summary: Decent soundboard recording with a few speed‑warps, featuring a modest first set and a punchier second set highlighted by Deal, Scarlet Fire and a fiery Wharf Rat.
review: The three reviewers agree the show is an average ’79 outing that picks up in the second set. Highlights such as “Deal,” the aggressive “Scarlet > Fire” sequence, “Let It Grow,” “Truckin’” and a raucous “Wharf Rat” are praised, while songs like “Around & Around” and the encore fall flat. The source is a soundboard (ladner/tetzeli fix) that runs slightly slow (~1 %) and shows tape‑warping and occasional battery‑wow noise, but overall remains clear enough for most listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed‑average with several strong moments; recording is generally good but has speed and tape‑warping issues, leading to a solid mid‑range overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Runs ~1 % slow (needs slight pitch up), exhibits tape‑slowdown/warp on early songs and occasional battery‑wow/AUD chatter, but overall clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as average and low‑energy, likely shortened to conserve stamina; the second set picks up with early peaks (Scarlet > Fire, Let It Grow) and maintains higher energy through drums/space, though it tapers off toward the encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Noted experimenting with his new “Tiger” guitar – aggressive tones on Deal and wild harmonic squeals during Scarlet Fire.
band_member_comments.Bob: Strong interplay with Jerry on Deal; vocal lead on Let It Grow; occasional criticism of his vocal style on Around & Around.
band_member_comments.Drums: High‑energy drums and space sections, described as sounding more like mid‑80s than typical ’79.
